title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
summary: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.
writeup: This interview features Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq, on the company's LPU architecture, a chip purpose-built for LLM inference rather than general-purpose GPU workloads. The discussion compares the LPU against GPUs using Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and covers technical design choices including static scheduling and the handling of mixture-of-experts models. Ross also addresses a strategic agreement with NVIDIA, alongside questions of local inference and the broader demand for compute.
